message:
Fix io_uring reactor spurious ERROR events from poll cancellations and stale fds

The CQE processing loop previously treated every negative result as a
blanket ERROR interest, which caused two categories of bugs:

1. PollRemove cancellations (ECANCELED/ENOENT) reuse the original
   token's user_data.  Surfacing these as ERROR readiness caused live
   registrations to see phantom error events after routine poll
   resubmissions.

2. Closed or invalidated file descriptors (EBADF/ENODEV) produced
   permanent ERROR completions that churned the event loop since the
   stale bookkeeping was never cleaned up.

Changes:
- Extract completion_errno(), is_poll_cancellation_errno(), and
  is_terminal_fd_errno() helpers for classifying negative CQE results
- Skip cancellation CQEs silently so they never become readiness events
- Prune registrations on terminal-fd errors to stop the churn cycle
- Only emit Event::errored for genuinely unexpected errors on still-
  registered tokens
- Move the positive-result path to call poll_mask_to_interest directly
  since all negative results are now handled above

Add test_poll_ignores_cancelled_poll_cqe_for_registered_token to verify
that explicitly cancelling a poll op does not surface a spurious ERROR
event on the still-live token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
